figures.caption: Figure 1: The accuracy vs. total cost trade-offs for a retrievalto-generation pipeline evaluated on the FinanceBench benchmark. The evaluation comprises 100 queries against hundreds of raw PDF documents, each containing over 100 pages of detailed financial reporting. Total cost includes LLM inference cost plus embedding cost (for Dense vector retrieval).

Figure 2: Users declare a logical pipeline, belief, and policy set through Credo APIs; the execution engine then adaptively rewrites and executes the pipeline based on evolving runtime beliefs and corresponding matched policies.
Figure 3: Example beliefs and policies for FinanceBench.

Figure 4: The Credo Web Interface. The UI visualizes the live execution trace and adaptive behavior of the system. 1. Controls & DB View: Select benchmark questions and toggle the database view to inspect persistent semantic state. 2. Compiled Plan: Displays the initial physical plan and parameters resolved before execution. 3. Execution History: Tracks the step-by-step evolution of the plan, highlighting corrective rewrites and parameter changes. 4. Belief Panel: Lists extracted semantic evidence (e.g., relevance, complexity) and their re-evaluation status. 5. Policy Panel: Shows active rules driving adaptation, allowing users to edit policies inline and observe immediate plan propagation.
